   "source": [
    "# Correlation Analysis: Statistical Methods\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/advanced_correlation.ipynb)\n",
    "\n",
    "gwexpy makes it easy to calculate statistical correlations between TimeSeries objects. This is useful for noise hunting and investigating nonlinear coupling.\n",
    "\n",
    "Supported methods:\n",
    "- **Pearson (PCC)**: Linear correlation.\n",
    "- **Kendall (Ktau)**: Rank correlation (robust to outliers, non-parametric).\n",
    "- **MIC**: Maximal Information Coefficient (robust to nonlinear relationships, requires `minepy` via `python scripts/install_minepy.py`)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "f78afc77",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:48:30.337457Z",

     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:48:39.698810Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:48:39.698108Z"
    },

   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import sys\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "\n",
    "# Ensure the root directory is in sys.path\n",
    "root = Path.cwd()\n",
    "while root.parent != root:\n",
    "    if (root / \"gwexpy\").exists():\n",
    "        if str(root) not in sys.path:\n",
    "            sys.path.insert(0, str(root))\n",
    "        break\n",
    "    root = root.parent\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.plot import PairPlot, Plot\n",
    "from gwexpy.timeseries import TimeSeries, TimeSeriesMatrix"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a1dbd24c",
   "metadata": {

     "status": "completed"

    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## Pairwise Correlation"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "b0199ad5",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:48:39.828549Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:48:39.826889Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:00.616331Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:00.611280Z"
    },

     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:48:39.806125+00:00",
     "status": "completed"

      ]

     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Create dummy data\n",
    "t = np.linspace(0, 10, 1000)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Linear relationship\n",
    "ts_a = TimeSeries(t, dt=0.01, name=\"A\")\n",
    "ts_b = TimeSeries(t * 2 + np.random.normal(0, 1, 1000), dt=0.01, name=\"B_Linear\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Nonlinear relationship (sine wave)\n",
    "ts_c = TimeSeries(np.sin(t) * 10, dt=0.01, name=\"C_Sine\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Random noise\n",
    "ts_d = TimeSeries(np.random.normal(0, 1, 1000), dt=0.01, name=\"D_Noise\")\n",
    "\n",
    "Plot(ts_a, ts_b, ts_c, ts_d, separate=True, sharex=True);"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "a4813048",
   "metadata": {

   "source": [
    "# Visualization\n",
    "pair = PairPlot([ts_a, ts_b, ts_c, ts_d], corner=True)\n",
    "pair.show()"

   "source": [
    "> **Note — automatic resampling**: If two time series have different sample rates, gwexpy will automatically resample one to match the other and emit a `UserWarning`. To suppress this, ensure both series share the same sample rate before calling correlation methods."
   ]
  },

   ]

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "cee6f6a3",
   "metadata": {

     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:03.868784+00:00",

   "source": [
    "## Correlation Vector (Noise Hunting)\n",
    "\n",
    "When investigating noise sources, we often want to check correlations between a target channel (e.g., DARM) and hundreds of auxiliary channels.\n",
    "`TimeSeriesMatrix.correlation_vector` efficiently computes this ranking."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "03d4f20e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.029455Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.028902Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.052395Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.049230Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.062639,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.057741+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:03.995102+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Create a Matrix with many auxiliary channels\n",
    "n_channels = 20\n",
    "data = np.random.randn(n_channels, 1, 1000)\n",
    "names = [f\"AUX-{i:02d}\" for i in range(n_channels)]\n",
    "\n",
    "# Inject signals into AUX-05 and AUX-12\n",
    "target_signal = np.sin(np.linspace(0, 20, 1000))\n",
    "data[5, 0, :] += target_signal * 5  # Strong coupling\n",
    "data[12, 0, :] += target_signal**2 * 5  # Nonlinear coupling\n",
    "\n",
    "matrix = TimeSeriesMatrix(data, dt=0.01, channel_names=names)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Target channel\n",
    "target = TimeSeries(\n",
    "    target_signal + np.random.normal(0, 0.1, 1000), dt=0.01, name=\"TARGET\"\n",
    ")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "f8c765d2",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.145806Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.145517Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.170026Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.168666Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.072485,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.171581+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:04.099096+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Computing MIC vector (Top 5)...\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/markdown": [
       "\n",
       "    **Note**: Skipping MIC example because minepy is not installed.\n",
       "\n",
       "    "
      ],
      "text/plain": [
       "<IPython.core.display.Markdown object>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Compute correlation vector\n",
    "# Use 'mic' to capture both linear and nonlinear (slower but powerful)\n",
    "# Use 'pearson' for speed\n",
    "\n",
    "try:\n",
    "    print(\"Computing MIC vector (Top 5)...\")\n",
    "    df_mic = matrix.correlation_vector(target, method=\"mic\", parallel=2)\n",
    "    print(df_mic.head(5))\n",
    "except ImportError:\n",
    "    from IPython.display import Markdown, display\n",
    "    display(Markdown(\"\"\"\n",
    "    **Note**: Skipping MIC example because minepy is not installed.\n",
    "\n",
    "    \"\"\"))\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",

   ],
   "source": [
    "print(\"Computing Pearson vector (Top 5)...\")\n",
    "df_pcc = matrix.correlation_vector(target, method=\"pearson\", parallel=1)\n",
    "print(df_pcc.head(5))"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "# Visualize ranking (Top 10)\n",
    "df_plot = df_pcc.head(10).iloc[::-1]  # Reverse to descending order\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
    "plt.barh(df_plot[\"channel\"], np.abs(df_plot[\"score\"]), color=\"skyblue\")\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Absolute Correlation Score (Pearson)\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"Top 10 Correlated Channels\")\n",
    "plt.grid(axis=\"x\", linestyle=\"--\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a063afeb",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.029212,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.168487+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.139275+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [

   },
   "source": [
    "## Partial Correlation (Confound Control)\n",
    "\n",
    "Partial correlation estimates direct coupling by removing shared confounds.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "818cdab3",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.281956Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.281716Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.299631Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.297663Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.047551,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.302837+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.255286+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "corr(x,y): 0.9809075921667307\n",
      "partial corr(x,y|z): 0.00441045286024793\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.timeseries import TimeSeries\n",
    "\n",
    "rng = np.random.default_rng(0)\n",
    "t = np.linspace(0, 10, 1000)\n",
    "z = np.sin(2 * np.pi * 0.5 * t) + 0.1 * rng.standard_normal(t.size)\n",
    "x = z + 0.1 * rng.standard_normal(t.size)\n",
    "y = z + 0.1 * rng.standard_normal(t.size)\n",
    "\n",
    "ts_x = TimeSeries(x, dt=t[1] - t[0], name=\"x\")\n",
    "ts_y = TimeSeries(y, dt=t[1] - t[0], name=\"y\")\n",
    "ts_z = TimeSeries(z, dt=t[1] - t[0], name=\"z\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"corr(x,y):\", ts_x.correlation(ts_y, method=\"pearson\"))\n",
    "print(\"partial corr(x,y|z):\", ts_x.partial_correlation(ts_y, controls=ts_z, method=\"residual\"))\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "7e9c6f47",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.024757,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.356011+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.331254+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## Association Edges and Graphs\n",
    "\n",
    "Use a target TimeSeries against a TimeSeriesMatrix to build edges.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 12,
   "id": "c0aaa42e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.473096Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.472511Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.513880Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.512464Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.077736,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.517821+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:05.440085+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Association edges:\n",
      "    row  col channel     score source target\n",
      "0    0    0       x  1.000000      x      x\n",
      "1    2    0       z  0.989968      x      z\n",
      "2    1    0       y  0.980908      x      y\n",
      "Partial correlation matrix:\n",
      " [[1.         0.1216813  0.6444092 ]\n",
      " [0.1216813  1.         0.67131295]\n",
      " [0.6444092  0.67131295 1.        ]]\n",
      "Graph built successfully.\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "from gwexpy.analysis import association_edges, build_graph\n",
    "from gwexpy.timeseries import TimeSeriesMatrix\n",
    "\n",
    "matrix = TimeSeriesMatrix(\n",
    "    np.stack([x, y, z], axis=0)[:, None, :],\n",
    "    dt=t[1] - t[0],\n",
    "    channel_names=[\"x\", \"y\", \"z\"],\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "edges = association_edges(ts_x, matrix, method=\"pearson\", topk=3)\n",
    "print(\"Association edges:\\n\", edges)\n",
    "\n",
    "pcorr = matrix.partial_correlation_matrix(shrinkage=\"auto\")\n",
    "print(\"Partial correlation matrix:\\n\", pcorr)\n",
    "\n",
    "# If networkx is available:\n",
    "graph = build_graph(edges, backend=\"none\")\n",
    "print(\"Graph built successfully.\")"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "# Bar plot of top edges\n",
    "top = edges.sort_values('score', ascending=False, key=abs).head(10)\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(6, 3))\n",
    "plt.barh(top['target'], top['score'])\n",
    "plt.gca().invert_yaxis()\n",
    "plt.xlabel('score')\n",
    "plt.title('Top associations')\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Heatmap of partial correlation\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(4, 3))\n",
    "plt.imshow(pcorr, vmin=-1, vmax=1, cmap='coolwarm')\n",
    "plt.colorbar(mappable=plt.gca().get_images()[-1] if plt.gca().get_images() else plt.gca().collections[-1], label='partial corr')\n",
    "plt.title('Partial correlation matrix')\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## FastMI (Experimental)\n",
    "\n",
    "FastMI is a fast mutual-information (MI) estimator based on a copula formulation (empirical CDF), a probit transform, and an FFT-based self-consistent density estimate.\n",
    "\n",
    "- API: `TimeSeries.fastmi(other, grid_size=..., quantile=..., eps=...)` or `correlation(other, method=\"fastmi\")`\n",
    "- Output: MI in **nats** (>= 0). Larger means stronger dependence (linear or nonlinear).\n",
    "- Parameters:\n",
    "  - `grid_size`: FFT grid resolution (trade-off: accuracy vs speed).\n",
    "  - `quantile`: trims extreme probit tails for numerical stability.\n",
    "  - `eps`: floor for probabilities/densities to avoid `log(0)`.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 14,
   "id": "e1e3a05b",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:06.918257Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:06.917941Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:06.993660Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:06.987226Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.114932,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:06.994672+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:06.879740+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "fastMI(x,y): 0.08925170168547956\n",
      "fastMI(x,z): 0.11320579203180092\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# fastMI (copula/probit + FFT-based MI estimator)\n",
    "# NOTE: this can be slower than Pearson for small n, but is useful for nonlinear dependence.\n",
    "print('fastMI(x,y):', ts_x.fastmi(ts_y, grid_size=128))\n",
    "print('fastMI(x,z):', ts_x.fastmi(ts_z, grid_size=128))\n"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "# Advanced Statistics\n",
    "\n",
    "gwexpy provides advanced statistical capabilities not only for correlation but also for examining data distribution shape and causality.\n",
    "\n",
    "- **Skewness**: Asymmetry of the distribution.\n",
    "- **Kurtosis**: Heaviness of distribution tails (presence of outliers).\n",
    "- **Distance Correlation (dCor)**: Measure of nonlinear dependency.\n",
    "- **Granger Causality**: Causality between time series (contribution to prediction)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "98ac8cfd",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.0333,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:08.326700+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:08.293400+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## Detecting Non-Gaussian Noise (Skewness / Kurtosis)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 16,
   "id": "42d69520",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:08.480015Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:08.479478Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:28.956286Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:28.949202Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 20.52657,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:28.963448+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:08.436878+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 1200x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Gaussian:     Skewness=0.117, Kurtosis=0.066\n",
      "Non-Gaussian: Skewness=1.981, Kurtosis=5.379\n",
      "Note: Gaussian distributions have Skewness~0, Kurtosis~0 (Fisher definition).\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Generate Gaussian noise and non-Gaussian noise (exponential distribution)\n",
    "np.random.seed(42)\n",
    "gauss_noise = TimeSeries(np.random.normal(0, 1, 1000), dt=0.01, name=\"Gaussian\")\n",
    "exp_noise = TimeSeries(\n",
    "    np.random.exponential(1, 1000) - 1, dt=0.01, name=\"Non-Gaussian\"\n",
    ")  # Centered\n",
    "\n",
    "# Plot\n",
    "plot = exp_noise.plot(label=\"Non-Gaussian\")\n",
    "plot.gca().plot(gauss_noise, label=\"Gaussian\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "plot.gca().legend()\n",
    "plot.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Calculate statistics\n",
    "print(\n",
    "    f\"Gaussian:     Skewness={gauss_noise.skewness():.3f}, Kurtosis={gauss_noise.kurtosis():.3f}\"\n",
    ")\n",
    "print(\n",
    "    f\"Non-Gaussian: Skewness={exp_noise.skewness():.3f}, Kurtosis={exp_noise.kurtosis():.3f}\"\n",
    ")\n",
    "print(\"Note: Gaussian distributions have Skewness~0, Kurtosis~0 (Fisher definition).\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8c0ef882",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.076169,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.098337+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.022168+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## Detecting Nonlinear Dependency (Distance Correlation)\n",
    "\n",
    "Let's examine the relationship between the sine wave data (`ts_c`) and linear data (`ts_a`) using dCor. We can detect relationships that Pearson correlation cannot capture."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 17,
   "id": "5e3b25af",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.366266Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.365998Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.391430Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.385947Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.116129,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.398875+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.282746+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/markdown": [
       "\n",
       "    **Note**: dcor package is not installed. Install it with: pip install dcor\n",
       "\n",
       "    "
      ],
      "text/plain": [
       "<IPython.core.display.Markdown object>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "try:\n",
    "    dcor_val = ts_a.distance_correlation(ts_c)\n",
    "    print(f\"Distance Correlation (A vs C): {dcor_val:.3f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"Pearson Correlation  (A vs C): {ts_a.pcc(ts_c):.3f}\")\n",
    "except ImportError:\n",
    "    from IPython.display import Markdown, display\n",
    "    display(Markdown(\"\"\"\n",
    "    **Note**: dcor package is not installed. Install it with: pip install dcor\n",
    "\n",
    "    \"\"\"))\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b2c8b121",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.072504,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.530041+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.457537+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## Estimating Causality (Granger Causality)\n",
    "\n",
    "Tests whether past values of one time series help predict future values of another time series."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 18,
   "id": "8ba7a211",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.790398Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.790131Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.021140Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.020089Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.309854,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.029858+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:29.720004+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Granger Causality X -> Y (p-value): 0.0000 (Significant)\n",
      "Granger Causality Y -> X (p-value): 0.0907 \n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Generate data with causal relationship (X -> Y)\n",
    "np.random.seed(0)\n",
    "n = 200\n",
    "x_val = np.random.randn(n)\n",
    "y_val = np.zeros(n)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Y depends on the value of X one step before\n",
    "for i in range(1, n):\n",
    "    y_val[i] = 0.5 * y_val[i - 1] + 0.8 * x_val[i - 1] + 0.1 * np.random.randn()\n",
    "\n",
    "ts_x = TimeSeries(x_val, dt=1, name=\"Cause (X)\")\n",
    "ts_y = TimeSeries(y_val, dt=1, name=\"Effect (Y)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "try:\n",
    "    # Does X cause Y? (Does X help predict Y?) -> p-value should be small\n",
    "    p_xy = ts_y.granger_causality(ts_x, maxlag=5)\n",
    "    # Does Y cause X? -> p-value should be large\n",
    "    p_yx = ts_x.granger_causality(ts_y, maxlag=5)\n",
    "    print(\n",
    "        f\"Granger Causality X -> Y (p-value): {p_xy:.4f} {'(Significant)' if p_xy < 0.05 else ''}\"\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    print(\n",
    "        f\"Granger Causality Y -> X (p-value): {p_yx:.4f} {'(Significant)' if p_yx < 0.05 else ''}\"\n",
    "    )\n",
    "except ImportError:\n",
    "    from IPython.display import Markdown, display\n",
    "    display(Markdown(\"\"\"\n",
    "    **Note**: The statsmodels package is not installed. To perform Granger causality analysis:\n",
    "    ```bash\n",
    "    pip install statsmodels\n",
    "    ```\n",
    "    You can continue with the rest of the notebook.\n",
    "    \"\"\"))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "859a7e2d",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.071642,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.182956+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.111314+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## Fast Mutual Information (fastmi)\n",
    "\n",
    "`fastmi` is a fast mutual-information (MI) estimator based on a copula formulation, a probit transform, and an FFT-based density estimate. It captures complex nonlinear dependencies that Pearson correlation might miss."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 19,
   "id": "d418e69b",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.505770Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.505108Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.598565Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.595859Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.198129,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.601219+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T07:50:30.403090+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Mutual Information (x, y): 0.0195 nats\n",
      "Mutual Information (x, z): 0.0000 nats\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "ts_y_aligned = ts_y.resample(ts_x.sample_rate.value)\n",
    "ts_z_aligned = ts_z.resample(ts_x.sample_rate.value)\n",
    "\n",
    "mi_xy = ts_x.fastmi(ts_y_aligned, grid_size=128)\n",
    "mi_xz = ts_x.fastmi(ts_z_aligned, grid_size=128)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Mutual Information (x, y): {mi_xy:.4f} nats\")\n",
    "print(f\"Mutual Information (x, z): {mi_xz:.4f} nats\")"
   ]
  }
 ],
